steps.title: Identify the cross-text claim
steps.content: Text 1 establishes that Portuguese traders brought cassava to the West African coast in the 1500s, but inland communities had little contact with the coast — meaning cassava's spread was slow. Text 2 (implied by context) supports the claim that cassava took a long time to become significant in West African diets.

steps.title: Match the supported statement
steps.content: Choice A states that cassava did not become a significant crop until long after introduction — exactly the conclusion both texts support together. The other choices either contradict the texts (B, D) or introduce unsupported claims (C). That matches choice A — A is correct.
